column2: Initial call to clarify goals, success criteria, project plan

- Review existing dashboards (Tableau/Metabase examples)
- Understand personas & their data needs (Corporate, Plant, Purchasing)
- Map key use cases (Open Orders, Repair Status, Lead Time components, Cost Savings basics etc)
- Data field analysis: what data is available / what user needs
- Define drill-down flows & export expectations
- Early technical feasibility sync with engineering

Conduct 30–45 min interviews:
- How they currently view data
- Pain points with Tableau/Metabase
- What KPIs matter
- How often they check dashboards
- Preferred format (charts, tables, exports)
- Drill-down expectations

- Define dashboard structure & widget layout
- Select core MVP widgets (KPIs, charts, tables)
- Define role-based dashboard variations
- Define drill-down flows (widget → detail → export)
- Validation with team

- Analyze the existing order management system and how repair data is stored.
- Review existing backend logic to understand constraints and integration points.
- Access and explore the client’s database and backend to inspect the real schema and data flows.
- Define the data model, relationships, and required transformations for analytics.
- Determine which services will handle data processing at the database and API level.
- Establish the approach for multi-tenancy and secure data separation.

- Build visual dashboard concepts (3–4 key screens)
- Apply accessible, modern UI (enterprise SaaS style)
- Design chart styles, color coding, status indicators
- Create plant/corporate/purchasing dashboard variations
- Add sample data for realism
- Prepare “demo-ready” version to show pilot customer
